title: vatica congesta
intro: Vatica congesta, also known as Bintangor, is a species of tree in the family Dipterocarpaceae. It is native to Borneo and is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which contains a high concentration of tannins and flavonoids. The bark of Vatica congesta has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Vatica congesta can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, as well as being used in traditional medicine as a powder or ointment.
